time_period: Act I to Act II transition
dramatic_function: Remove Manimekalai from the city through sacred transport while preserving bodily vulnerability and refusing a measurable route
Make Manimekalai orient herself through tide sun birds wind and silence before sacred memory opens

location: Gomukhi pond and Buddha seat on Manipallavam
dramatic_function: Join sacred geography previous birth and acceptance of the Amudhasurabhi without turning the island into fantasy spectacle
Make the merchants' return to Aputhiran require navigation cost weather and responsibility rather than an instantaneous reversal

working_scene_title: The child entrusted to the sea
dramatic_function: Connect Naga Chola kinship merchant trust and maritime danger without fixing the island to a modern ethnic map
Present the lost child royal grief missed festival and destruction tradition as layered literary memory rather than archaeological reenactment presented as fact

location: Eastern coastal plain to Kaveri interior
dramatic_function: Move Manimekalai from maritime Puhar toward the Chera world through changing ecology labour and overland dependency
Establish Vanji as an inland Chera exchange and intellectual centre without declaring Karur the only possible literary identification
Reveal Kanchi's crisis through disrupted traffic migration weakened animals and news before the city appears
